sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check cursor position
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('Y', 'O'):
        b = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(r for r, c in b)
        max_row = max(r for r, c in b)
        min_col = min(c for r, c in b)
        max_col = max(c for r, c in b)
        print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, bbox=({min_row},{min_col})-({max_row},{max_col})")

# Let me also check what changed between previous and current frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_nodes = {n['color']: n for n in previous_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
    curr_nodes = {n['color']: n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
    for color in ('Y', 'O'):
        if color in prev_nodes and color in curr_nodes:
            p = prev_nodes[color]
            c = curr_nodes[color]
            pb = p['boundary']
            cb = c['boundary']
            print(f"Color {color}: prev={min(r for r,c in pb)},{min(c for r,c in pb)}-{max(r for r,c in pb)},{max(c for r,c in pb)} -> curr={min(r for r,c in cb)},{min(c for r,c in cb)}-{max(r for r,c in cb)},{max(c for r,c in cb)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The cursor didn't move at all. UP doesn't work from this position. Let me try a different approach. Maybe the game involves clicking on the panels with MOUSE to change the green squares. Let me look at the structure again:
- Upper panel: 3x7 grid with 4 green squares (N color) at positions (15,13), (15,19), (15,31), (15,43) - these are in row 2 of the grid
- Lower panel: 2x2 grid with 1 green square at (51,43) - bottom-left corner
Maybe the goal is to match the lower panel's pattern to something, or to move the green squares around. Let me try clicking on the green square in the lower panel with MOUSE.
Actually, let me first try to understand the black path structure better. The cursor is at (32,48)-(37,53). Looking at the image, the cursor is on the right end of the horizontal bar. There's a vertical bar going up from there. Let me try to trace the path:
Looking at the black nodes:
- id=3: B, pixels=412, bbox=(7,5)-(56,57) - this is the main black structure containing the upper panel
- id=36: B, pixels=53, bbox=(18,6)-(27,49) - this seems to be the left vertical bar
- id=45: b, pixels=2, bbox=(32,55)-(33,55) - small black piece near cursor
- id=47: b, pixels=11, bbox=(36,48)-(39,55) - small black piece below cursor
- id=49: b, pixels=27, bbox=(44,42)-(57,55) - this seems to be the lower panel border
Wait, I see "b" (blue) and "B" (black) as different colors. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the black path seems to be made of "B" (black) and "b" (blue) nodes. The cursor is on a path that connects the upper and lower panels.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe I should use MOUSE to click on specific cells in the panels. Let me try clicking on the green square in the lower panel first.
The lower panel green square is at bbox (51,43)-(54,46). Let me try clicking at row 52, col 44 (center of that square).

sections.content:

# Let's click at the center: row 52, col 44

</parameter>
</function>
